subtasks.description: Insert a <div id="summary-bar"> element between the task list and the bottom of .container. Add CSS styles: muted text color (#888), small font size (~0.85rem), centered, padding above, and ensure it remains visible on mobile viewports without scrolling.

Add a renderSummary() function that computes counts from the global tasks array (not filteredTasks): activeCount = tasks where !completed, completedCount = tasks where completed. Output text based on currentFilter: 'all' → 'X remaining · Y completed'; 'active' → 'X active tasks'; 'completed' → 'Y completed tasks'. When tasks.length === 0, show 'No tasks yet — add one above!' instead of counts.

Call renderSummary() at the end of the render() function so the bar updates on every state change. Update the #empty-msg text to be context-aware: when tasks.length === 0 show 'No tasks yet — add one above!'; when filtered view is empty but tasks exist show 'No tasks here.' (existing behavior).
Run AI review and prepare human handoff

status_history.message: AI review completed. Awaiting human review and PR decision.

validations: #summary-bar element inserted between task list and container bottom with correct CSS (muted color #888, 0.85rem, centered, padding-top: 16px)
renderSummary() computes activeCount/completedCount from global tasks array, not filteredTasks
Filter-aware text: 'all' shows 'X remaining · Y completed', 'active' shows 'X active task(s)', 'completed' shows 'Y completed task(s)'
renderSummary() called at end of render(), triggered by every state mutation (add, toggle, delete, filter change)
empty-msg updated to context-aware text: 'No tasks yet — add one above!' when tasks.length === 0, 'No tasks here.' when filtered view is empty
Singular/plural handled correctly (e.g., '1 active task' vs '2 active tasks')
Summary bar shows empty string when tasks.length === 0, avoiding duplicate empty state messaging
